chapters.title: Anyone can build a personal brand from scratch
chapters.text:
These days, most school-age kids dream of becoming YouTubers or social media stars. Even though personal branding is not yet part of the standard career day circuit, kids already know that making videos, posting short thoughts, and sharing images on Instagram is a real career path that can lead to both fame and fortune.

The internet has reshaped marketing, publishing, fashion, and entertainment, and it has given individuals the tools to build personal brands with global reach. Over the last decade, the influencer marketing industry has grown into a multibillion-dollar business.

Young people spend less time on traditional media and far more time on online content. As a result, brands have dramatically increased their social media budgets. For instance, TV viewership has been falling for years, while YouTube logged more than 1.25 billion hours of daily viewership in 2019. About one of every five minutes spent on mobile browsing goes to Facebook apps, and Instagram sees more than 66,000 new posts uploaded every minute.

The flood of social platforms has turned influencer marketing into a viable way for almost anyone to build an audience and earn a real income. In 2019, the highest earning YouTubers together pulled in 149 million dollars.

When you do what you love and earn your living from it, you are crushing it.

The good news is that anyone in any industry can build a personal brand, as long as they are willing to do the work. You do not have to stay hidden behind someone else name or logo until you have built up enough credibility to step out on your own. You can make your brand so strong that people will pay you for your products, your promotions, or simply for being you.

Read on to learn the core tactics for building a personal brand that pays.

chapters.title: Focus on the things that actually move the needle
chapters.text:
In the world of entrepreneurship, failure stories outnumber the wins. While some influencers have built personal brands that earn well, plenty of others have tried to follow the same path and fallen flat.

A lot of entrepreneurs shoot themselves in the foot with mistakes they make before they even start.

Most influencers fail because they spend their energy on the wrong things. They care, but not enough about the things that actually matter. So what really matters? Gary Vaynerchuk highlights seven vital elements of personal brand building, each explored later.

First, you need the right intent. People who chase influence to get rich quick rarely succeed. Real entrepreneurs are not in it just for the money. They want to add value to other people lives by sharing knowledge or providing great service. Wrong intent makes it almost impossible to build a strategy that lasts.

Second, you have to be authentic. Be honest with your audience and resist the urge to fake it. Authenticity is refreshing in this day and age. Your odds of success rise when you create and share from a place of genuine enthusiasm, rather than chasing fame and fast money.

Third, passion is essential. Your passion is what carries you through the hard stretches. Whether your thing is fashion or daily YouTube vlogs, real passion lifts the quality of what you put out and pulls in attention and goodwill.

chapters.title: Patience and passion always travel together
chapters.text:
Another key ingredient in building a brand that pays is patience. You may have to move more slowly than you would like, which means thinking in years, not weeks. Building something valuable from the ground up takes time, and there are no shortcuts. So settle in and be patient.

Speed is also part of the picture, and that does not contradict patience. Speed is for the short term, and patience is for the long term. As an entrepreneur, you need to live in do mode. Use your hours well and squeeze the most from your tools.

To build a thriving brand, you have to put leisure on hold at the start and pour yourself into the goal.

Hard work is another non-negotiable trait for entrepreneurs. As an influencer, you may need to cut down on random YouTube videos and long lunch breaks. Building a real business demands relentless focus.

The final element of brand success is attention. You have to stay alert and watch where the world is heading. Do not get so locked into one platform that you ignore the others.

A useful note is that entrepreneurs will face lean stretches with little or no income. If you lack a real passion for the work during those times, it is brutally hard to keep going.

chapters.title: Document your journey as you go
chapters.text:
The biggest reason people never start a personal brand is that when it is time to create, they freeze. Questions like what should I talk about or do I have anything worth sharing get in the way.

Building credibility is one of the toughest challenges for new influencers. Many people never get a brand off the ground because they cannot decide what to create. Questions like what kind of content should I produce and what topics should I cover become obstacles instead of fuel.

Gary Vaynerchuk has a simple answer for anyone stuck at this stage. Just start documenting. You do not have to wait until you can create perfect content. Great content depends on great storytelling, and your story is one of a kind, so you are the only one who can tell it.

Skip the pressure of crafting elaborate YouTube videos or rewriting the same Facebook post a dozen times. Instead, use every platform you have to document your real life and share your honest take. Give your audience a real look at who you are and let them watch your transformation unfold.

Documenting has freed me from the pressure of having to create all the time, Gary Vaynerchuk has said.

Use tools like Instagram Stories, Snapchat, YouTube videos, and Facebook Live to share a slice of your day. Walk your audience through your routines, share what you eat, and bring them along on your workouts.

Whether you are moving into a post-divorce apartment, starting college, or heading off on vacation, take your audience with you. You are the actor and the producer of this show.

Documenting is valuable because it shapes how people will see you five or ten years from now.

chapters.title: Do not let fear block you from creating
chapters.text:
Even when people know the seven elements of brand success, including intent, authenticity, virtue, patience, speed, passion, and hard work, some still cannot bring themselves to start. No amount of advice pushes them to act. They always have a reason to delay.

You do not get to call yourself an expert until you have put in the work, and the market decides that, not you, as Gary Vaynerchuk has said.

The excuses people use to avoid starting come from three main flavors of fear.

The first is the fear of failure. When you ask aspiring entrepreneurs why they have not started building a brand, many will say they are afraid it will not work. The truth is, these people are not scared of failure itself. They are scared of the judgment of people whose opinions matter to them. If that sounds like you, recognize that naysayers are part of every journey, and take ownership of your choices.

The second is the fear of wasting time. Starting a business takes time, and there is no debating that. Yes, you are busy, but is the busyness pointing you toward a life you actually want.

The time you invest in building your brand is not wasted. It is an investment in something you care about, and that thing can bring you joy. If your current life is not delivering that, taking a chance on a path that might is always worth it.

Everyone looks arrogant until they pioneer something new.

The third fear is looking vain. When you first become an influencer, especially while you document your journey, people may call you shallow or narcissistic. They may even say you are full of yourself for walking around with a camera in your face.

Be bold and ignore the vain label. Embrace it the way everyone else who is crushing it does, and keep going. Remember, successful entrepreneurs base their key decisions on their convictions, not on what other people think.

chapters.title: Use Instagram direct messages to grow fast
chapters.text:
Instagram, second only to YouTube, has launched countless people to fame. It is a massive platform where creators can hit it big, and it is currently the top network for both reach and impact among social media.

Instagram has a long list of features that matter to budding influencers and entrepreneurs. You can shape your content in many ways, drop a link in your bio, and steer visitors to your website, blog, or other social profiles.

Even though Instagram reaches a giant audience, the platform is built on visual content.

Whether you are a photographer, chef, designer, or artist, you can build awareness on the platform through hashtags, collaborations, tags, and ads.

Instagram is perfect for strengthening your brand. If you have not set up a profile yet, do it soon or you may regret the delay.

Here is a step by step guide to building a brand and growing a business on Instagram.

Make sure your account showcases top tier visual content, and aim for the highest quality you can manage. Run keyword searches tied to your niche, like motorcycles if you are building a biker brand. Pick the first hashtag in the search results and look through its related images. Check each account and the linked websites to confirm they belong to businesses in your space. Write personal direct messages to the owners, and avoid copy and paste templates. Explain clearly what caught your eye about them, why you deserve a moment of their time, and what value you bring.

Set aside at least six to seven hours a day for searching, clicking, investigating, and direct messaging. Only a small number of people will reply, and that is all you need.

With each solid interaction, you grow your visibility and show you are worth noticing. Stick with this approach, and you will soon find that you are the brand people reach out to.

chapters.title: Facebook is more versatile than its reputation suggests
chapters.text:
Despite the constant talk of its decline, Facebook is still a giant in social media, with around 2 billion monthly active users, putting it on par with YouTube for personal brand building and income generation.

Gary Vaynerchuk stresses that a Facebook page is a must if you want to build and monetize a brand. Even if your business is thriving elsewhere, a solid Facebook strategy is essential for reaching your full potential.

What sets Facebook apart is its flexibility. Long written posts and images do not always fit on YouTube, and Instagram main feed only hosts videos up to a minute. Long form writing will never catch on on Snapchat.

Unlike platforms that limit the content you can post, Facebook handles everything from a 13 paragraph blog post to images and SoundCloud audio. Facebook gives you creative flexibility and the best ad targeting in the business.

Aim your brand at the right social platforms to grow and earn well.

Facebook versatility also extends to advertising, with unmatched ad targeting precision. That makes it ideal for beginners. Facebook lets you target by interests, zip codes, or employers, and even a small budget, like 13 dollars for a fashion brand post, can pull in about 2,600 views if your targeting is on point.

So if you do not have a Facebook fan page yet, set one up today. Reach a giant pool of nearly 2 billion monthly users and grab the opportunities that come with that kind of attention.

chapters.title: Give YouTube a real shot
chapters.text:
As an influencer, consider giving YouTube a try, even if you do not see yourself as video material. A lot of people avoid YouTube because they think they are not camera worthy. The truth is, you do not have to be glamorous or conventionally special to be a vlogger.

Far from the polished beauty influencers and rising pop stars, most YouTubers are everyday people. Vloggers come in all ages, body types, and even include people with disabilities or visible differences. Vlogging is more about documenting than creating, which makes it accessible to nearly everyone.

Remember, you are not creating when you are documenting, so you can learn on the fly. You do not need to be a pro to make it on YouTube.

There is an audience for every niche on YouTube, no matter how small or seemingly dull. Interesting is subjective, so do not worry about whether your topic will grab anyone. Search for garage sale on YouTube and you will find hundreds of videos with view counts ranging from 50,000 to 137,000.

Start first, then adjust as you go.

With YouTube, you have nothing to lose. Spend a year learning the platform, testing different formats, and paying attention to how the audience responds. Even if the verdict is that your content is not exciting, you will have learned whether vlogging is right for you, and you can pivot to something else.

chapters.title: Final thoughts on building a personal brand
chapters.text:
Building a personal brand is something anyone can pull off, provided they are ready to put in the work. And with the rise of the internet and social media, personal branding is easier than it has ever been.

As people spend more of their lives online, especially on social media, influencer marketing has become a reliable and very profitable path for those building an online profile. Anyone can build a real following and earn a fortune through social media. Plenty of people have used the internet to build personal brands, thriving businesses, and the lives of their dreams, and you can do the same.

You can make your brand so strong that people will happily pay for your products or services, or for promoting their products and services, and they will even pay you simply for being you. In the online world, social media is as much about entertainment as it is about selling something or offering a service.

All it takes is picking one thing you love that adds value to others and building a business around it. You also need the right intent. Trying to become an influencer just to get rich fast is a flawed approach that usually ends in failure. Your main goal should be to help people, provide value, and offer great services or knowledge. With the right intent, financial success tends to follow.

A few ideas to try next include being authentic and real with your audience, lining your brand up with your passions since passion is what keeps you going until you see results, and choosing the right social media platform for the audience you want to reach.

If you made it to the end, well done.
